result.stdout:
Description:
  Microsoft.DotNet.ApiCompat v11.0.100-preview.2.26159.112+17d11de66cf75b962995c81dd1235fae9aa5ece0

Usage:
  Microsoft.DotNet.ApiCompat.Tool [command] [options]

Options:
  --generate-suppression-file                                                          If true, generates a compatibility suppression file.
  --preserve-unnecessary-suppressions                                                  If true, preserves unnecessary suppressions when re-generating the suppression file.
  --permit-unnecessary-suppressions                                                    If true, permits unnecessary suppressions in the suppression file.
  --suppression-file <file>                                                            The path to one or more suppression files to read from.
  --suppression-output-file <suppression-output-file>                                  The path to a suppression file to write to when --generate-suppression-file is true.
  --noWarn <noWarn>                                                                    A NoWarn string that allows to disable specific rules.
  --respect-internals                                                                  If true, includes both internal and public API.
  --roslyn-assemblies-path <file>                                                      The path to the directory that contains the Microsoft.CodeAnalysis assemblies.
  -v, --verbosity <High|Low|Normal>                                                    Controls the log level verbosity. Allowed values are high, normal, and low. [default: Normal]
  --enable-rule-attributes-must-match                                                  If true, enables rule to check that attributes match.
  --exclude-attributes-file <exclude-attributes-file>                                  The path to one or more attribute exclusion files with types in DocId format.
  --enable-rule-cannot-change-parameter-name                                           If true, enables rule to check that the parameter names between public methods do not change.
  -l, --left, --left-assembly <left-assembly> (REQUIRED)                               The path to one or more assemblies that serve as the left side to compare.
  -r, --right, --right-assembly <right-assembly> (REQUIRED)                            The path to one or more assemblies that serve as the right side to compare.
  --strict-mode                                                                        If true, performs api compatibility checks in strict mode
  --left-assembly-references, --lref <file1,file2,...>                                 Paths to assembly references or the underlying directories for a given left. Values must be separated by commas: ','.
  --right-assembly-references, --rref <file1,file2,...>                                Paths to assembly references or the underlying directories for a given right. Values must be separated by commas: ','.
  --create-work-item-per-assembly                                                      If true, enqueues a work item per passed in left and right assembly.
  --left-assemblies-transformation-pattern <left-assemblies-transformation-pattern>    A transformation pattern for the left side assemblies.
  --right-assemblies-transformation-pattern <right-assemblies-transformation-pattern>  A transformation pattern for the right side assemblies.
  -?, -h, --help                                                                       Show help and usage information
  --version                                                                            Show version information

Commands:
  package <--package>  Validates the compatibility of package assets

Usage:
  Microsoft.DotNet.ApiCompat.Tool [command] [options]

Options:
  --generate-suppression-file                                                          If true, generates a compatibility suppression file.
  --preserve-unnecessary-suppressions                                                  If true, preserves unnecessary suppressions when re-generating the suppression file.
  --permit-unnecessary-suppressions                                                    If true, permits unnecessary suppressions in the suppression file.
  --suppression-file <file>                                                            The path to one or more suppression files to read from.
  --suppression-output-file <suppression-output-file>                                  The path to a suppression file to write to when --generate-suppression-file is true.
  --noWarn <noWarn>                                                                    A NoWarn string that allows to disable specific rules.
  --respect-internals                                                                  If true, includes both internal and public API.
  --roslyn-assemblies-path <file>                                                      The path to the directory that contains the Microsoft.CodeAnalysis assemblies.
  -v, --verbosity <High|Low|Normal>                                                    Controls the log level verbosity. Allowed values are high, normal, and low. [default: Normal]
  --enable-rule-attributes-must-match                                                  If true, enables rule to check that attributes match.
  --exclude-attributes-file <exclude-attributes-file>                                  The path to one or more attribute exclusion files with types in DocId format.
  --enable-rule-cannot-change-parameter-name                                           If true, enables rule to check that the parameter names between public methods do not change.
  -l, --left, --left-assembly <left-assembly> (REQUIRED)                               The path to one or more assemblies that serve as the left side to compare.
  -r, --right, --right-assembly <right-assembly> (REQUIRED)                            The path to one or more assemblies that serve as the right side to compare.
  --strict-mode                                                                        If true, performs api compatibility checks in strict mode
  --left-assembly-references, --lref <file1,file2,...>                                 Paths to assembly references or the underlying directories for a given left. Values must be separated by commas: ','.
  --right-assembly-references, --rref <file1,file2,...>                                Paths to assembly references or the underlying directories for a given right. Values must be separated by commas: ','.
  --create-work-item-per-assembly                                                      If true, enqueues a work item per passed in left and right assembly.
  --left-assemblies-transformation-pattern <left-assemblies-transformation-pattern>    A transformation pattern for the left side assemblies.
  --right-assemblies-transformation-pattern <right-assemblies-transformation-pattern>  A transformation pattern for the right side assemblies.
  -?, -h, --help                                                                       Show help and usage information
  --version                                                                            Show version information

Commands:
  package <--package>  Validates the compatibility of package assets

result.stdout:
Description:
  Validates the compatibility of package assets

Usage:
  Microsoft.DotNet.ApiCompat.Tool package <--package> [options]

Arguments:
  <--package>  The path to the package that should be validated

Options:
  --runtime-graph <json>                                                           The path to the runtime graph to read from.
  --run-api-compat                                                                 If true, performs api compatibility checks on the package assets.
  --enable-strict-mode-for-compatible-tfms                                         Validates api compatibility in strict mode for contract and implementation assemblies for all compatible target frameworks.
  --enable-strict-mode-for-compatible-frameworks-in-package                        Validates api compatibility in strict mode for assemblies that are compatible based on their target framework.
  --enable-strict-mode-for-baseline-validation                                     Validates api compatibility in strict mode for package baseline checks.
  --baseline-package <nupkg>                                                       The path to a baseline package to validate against the current package.
  --package-assembly-references <tfm=file1,file2,...>                              Paths to assembly references or their underlying directories for a specific target framework in the package. Values must be separated by commas: ','.
  --baseline-package-assembly-references <tfm=file1,file2,...>                     Paths to assembly references or their underlying directories for a specific target framework in the baseline package. Values must be separated by commas: ','.
  --baseline-package-frameworks-to-ignore <baseline-package-frameworks-to-ignore>  Target frameworks to ignore from the baseline package. Supports the wildcard character '*' at the end of the string. Culture and casing is ignored. The framework string must match the folder name in the baseline package.
  -?, -h, --help                                                                   Show help and usage information
  --generate-suppression-file                                                      If true, generates a compatibility suppression file.
  --preserve-unnecessary-suppressions                                              If true, preserves unnecessary suppressions when re-generating the suppression file.
  --permit-unnecessary-suppressions                                                If true, permits unnecessary suppressions in the suppression file.
  --suppression-file <file>                                                        The path to one or more suppression files to read from.
  --suppression-output-file <suppression-output-file>                              The path to a suppression file to write to when --generate-suppression-file is true.
  --noWarn <noWarn>                                                                A NoWarn string that allows to disable specific rules.
  --respect-internals                                                              If true, includes both internal and public API.
  --roslyn-assemblies-path <file>                                                  The path to the directory that contains the Microsoft.CodeAnalysis assemblies.
  -v, --verbosity <High|Low|Normal>                                                Controls the log level verbosity. Allowed values are high, normal, and low. [default: Normal]
  --enable-rule-attributes-must-match                                              If true, enables rule to check that attributes match.
  --exclude-attributes-file <exclude-attributes-file>                              The path to one or more attribute exclusion files with types in DocId format.
  --enable-rule-cannot-change-parameter-name                                       If true, enables rule to check that the parameter names between public methods do not change.
